content.full_text:
93-2623

FRPC

MAY 07 1992

JEFFREY W. KRAMER
1801 CENTURY PARK EAST, 16TH FLOOR
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90067
TELEPHONE (213) 553-4441

May 1, 1992


Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

     Re:  Request For Opinion Or Written Advice
          Pursuant To Gov. Code §83114

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     The purpose of this letter is to make a correction
regarding the facts stated in my April 24, 1992 letter to you
requesting an opinion or written advice pursuant to
Government Code section 83114 regarding my ability to
participate as a councilmember in a decision the Malibu City
Council may be making regarding its City Attorney.

     With reference to the numbered paragraphs 7 and 10 in my
April 24, 1992 letter, I have been advised that our firm has
this past month again been called upon by the City of
Industry to perform a small amount of additional follow-up
work relating to the August 21, 1991 settlement.

     Should you require any additional information, please
let me know.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Jeffrey W. Kramer
                                        for
                                        TROY & GOULD

JWK/jhs

JEFFREY W. KRAMER
1801 CENTURY PARK EAST, 16TH FLOOR
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90067
TELEPHONE (213) 553-4441


April 24, 1992


VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Scott Hallabrin, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J. Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

     Re: Request For Opinion Or Written Advice
         Pursuant To Gov. Code §83114

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     I am a councilmember of the City of Malibu and I am
writing to request an opinion or written advice pursuant to
Government Code section 83114 regarding my ability to
participate in a decision the Malibu City Council may be
making in one of its upcoming meetings.  Because the City
Council would like to consider this matter as soon as
possible, your early response to this request would be
greatly appreciated.

     My question is whether, based on the facts described in
this letter, the Fair Political Practices Act would prohibit
me from voting upon or participating in any decision by the
Malibu City Council to hire as the City Attorney for Malibu
an attorney with whom I have had a previous professional
affiliation.

     The facts are as follows:

     1.     On April 21, 1992, I was sworn in as a new member
of the City Council of the City of Malibu, having won

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 24, 1992
Page 2


election to this position at a general municipal election
occurring on April 14, 1992.

     2.     The City Council may consider hiring a new City
Attorney, and one candidate for the position of City Attorney
may be Graham A. Ritchie of Simmons, Ritchie, Segal and Stark
("Simmons, Ritchie").

     3.     Prior to the March 28, 1991 incorporation of the
City of Malibu, Mr. Ritchie and his firm and I and my firm,
Troy & Gould, served as legal co-counsel for the Malibu
Committee For Incorporation ("MCI"), and jointly handled
litigation against the County of Los Angeles and other
parties relating to the incorporation. Mr. Ritchie's firm
and my firm had separate fee arrangements with MCI. In the
case of Troy & Gould, all of my services were provided free
of charge and the firm charged only for the services of other
attorneys and paralegals and also for costs. I have no
knowledge of the fee arrangement made by Mr. Ritchie's firm,
although I understand that Mr. Ritchie also represented MCI
entirely or primarily on a pro bono basis.

     4.     Mr. Ritchie through his firm serves as the City
Attorney for the City of Industry.

     5.     In April 1990, Troy & Gould was retained by the
City of Industry and two of its agencies (the Industry Urban
Development Agency and the Civic-Recreation Industrial
Authority) to serve as co-counsel with Simmons, Ritchie in
the defense of a civil action filed in the Los Angeles
Superior Court (Case No. C 757 925). A copy of the retainer
agreement is enclosed for your reference.

     6.     In June 1990, the City of Industry and one of its
agencies (the Industry Urban-Development Agency) retained
Troy & Gould to represent them in another civil action filed
in the Los Angeles Superior Court (Case No. KC 000 669). A
reprint of the retainer agreement for this representation is
enclosed for your reference. Mr. Ritchie was named as a
defendant in that action and was separately represented by
the Los Angeles law firm of Munger, Tolles & Olson.

     7.     On August 21, 1991, both lawsuits (Case No. C 757
925 and Case No. KC 000 669) were settled and Troy & Gould's
representation of the City of Industry and its agencies came

Fair Political Practices Commission
April 24, 1992
Page 3


to an end, with the exception of a couple of small follow-up
matters. The last date of any legal service rendered by Troy
& Gould to the City of Industry or any of its agencies was
January 17, 1992, and the last payment received by Troy &
Gould from the City of Industry or any of its agencies was a
payment of $165 received on March 2, 1992. There are no
outstanding invoices or other obligations between Troy &
Gould and the City of Industry or any of its agencies.

     8. I am advised that the decisions by the City of
Industry to retain my law firm were taken by a vote of the
City Council, based on Mr. Ritchie's recommendations, and
that Mr. Ritchie himself neither had nor has authority to
retain our firm on behalf of the City of Industry.

     9. Neither Mr. Ritchie nor Simmons, Ritchie have ever
retained my services or the services of my law firm.

     10. Neither my law firm nor I are presently performing
any work for the City of Industry or any of its agencies.

     Should you require any further information, please do
not hesitate to call me. I look forward to your response.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   Jeffrey W. Kramer

JWK/jhs

sections.question: My question is whether, based on the facts described in this letter, the Fair Political Practices Act would prohibit me from voting upon or participating in any decision by the Malibu City Council to hire as the City Attorney for Malibu an attorney with whom I have had a previous professional affiliation.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a newly elected Malibu City Council member vote on hiring a City Attorney with whom the councilmember's law firm previously served as co-counsel on incorporation litigation and co-counsel on subsequent City of Industry defense litigation, particularly given that the candidate currently serves as City Attorney for the City of Industry?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated April 24, 1992, with a May 6, 1992 supplemental correction letter. No FPPC response/advice letter is included in this document. The request contains detailed facts numbered 1-10 describing the professional relationships at issue. The supplemental letter references a correction regarding paragraphs 7 and 10 regarding additional City of Industry work performed in the past month.

embedding.summary: A newly sworn-in Malibu City Council member requests FPPC advice on whether he may participate in a vote to hire a candidate for City Attorney with whom his law firm previously had professional relationships, including serving as co-counsel on incorporation litigation and on City of Industry defense litigation. A supplemental letter corrects the initial submission to note additional recent follow-up work performed for the City of Industry.
